# Template Code / Unused Code
## ART Anova Template Code
```{r, eval=FALSE}
# Analysis of Variance

# because these data are not normal, I am using an Aligned Ranks Anova method from Steve (found in the file "Nonparametric stats_forEmma.Rmd")

library(ARTool)

## Edge vs Interior
perc_occluded_other_dfe_aovART <- art(perc_occluded_other~dfe, data = LeafSEMxUNESites)
anova(perc_occluded_other_dfe_aovART)

# p = 0.95567

## Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by dist to Boston)

perc_occluded_other_dist_aovART <- art(perc_occluded_other~urb_cat_dist*dfe, data = LeafSEMxUNESites)
anova(perc_occluded_other_dist_aovART)

# Urban vs. Rural (dist): p = 0.41327  
# Interaction (dist): p = 0.87653

## Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by %ISA)

perc_occluded_other_isa_aovART <- art(perc_occluded_other~urb_cat_isa*dfe, data = LeafSEMxUNESites)
anova(perc_occluded_other_isa_aovART)

# Urban vs. Rural (ISA): p = 0.12090 
# Interaction (ISA): p = 0.70138
```

## Code for making a glm loop (not used)
```{r, eval=FALSE}      
glm_pvals <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=12, ncol=6)
colnames(glm_pvals) <- c("Response_Variable","Edge_vs_Interior","Urb_v_Rural_Dist","Interaction_Dist","Urb_v_Rural_ISA","Interaction_ISA" )
glm_pvals[,1] <- c("perc_occluded")


for(i in 1:13) {
  
# Edge vs. Interior
X_glm_EvI <- glm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites[[i]]~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites,family = poisson())
X_glm_EvI_sum <- summary(X_glm_EvI)
X_glm_EvI_pval <- X_glm_EvI_sum$coefficients["dfe90","Pr(>|z|)"] # this extracts the p-value for edge vs. interior
glm_poi_pvals[i,2] <- X_glm_EvI_pval # this places it into the matrix we made

# Urban vs. Rural and Interaction Term (by distance from Boston)
X_glm_dist <- glm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites[[i]]~urb_cat_dist*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites,family = poisson())
X_glm_dist_sum <- summary(X_glm_dist)
U_R_glm_dist_pval <- X_glm_dist_sum$coefficients["urb_cat_distUrban","Pr(>|z|)"] # this extracts the p-value for urban vs. rural
Int_glm_dist_pval <- X_glm_dist_sum$coefficients["urb_cat_distUrban:dfe90","Pr(>|z|)"] # this extracts the p-value for the interaction term
glm_poi_pvals[i,3] <- U_R_glm_dist_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
glm_poi_pvals[i,4] <- Int_glm_dist_pval # this places it into the matrix we made

# Urban vs. Rural and Interaction Term (by %ISA)
X_glm_isa <- glm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites[[i]]~urb_cat_isa*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites,family = poisson())
X_glm_isa_sum <- summary(X_glm_isa)
U_R_glm_isa_pval <- X_glm_isa_sum$coefficients["urb_cat_isaUrban","Pr(>|z|)"] # this extracts the p-value for urban vs. rural
Int_glm_isa_pval <- X_glm_isa_sum$coefficients["urb_cat_isaUrban:dfe90","Pr(>|z|)"] # this extracts the p-value for the interaction term
glm_poi_pvals[i,5] <- U_R_glm_isa_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
glm_poi_pvals[i,6] <- Int_glm_isa_pval # this places it into the matrix we made

}

# this is our matrix! now we make it into a data frame
glm_poi_pvals <- as.data.frame(glm_poi_pvals)

# make sure all the numbers are classified as "numeric"
glm_poi_pvals$Edge_vs_Interior <- as.numeric(glm_poi_pvals$Edge_vs_Interior)
glm_poi_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_Dist <- as.numeric(glm_poi_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_Dist)
glm_poi_pvals$Interaction_Dist <- as.numeric(glm_poi_pvals$Interaction_Dist)
glm_poi_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_ISA <- as.numeric(glm_poi_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_ISA)
glm_poi_pvals$Interaction_ISA <- as.numeric(glm_poi_pvals$Interaction_ISA)

# reduce all p-values in the matrix to two decimal points
glm_poi_pvals[] <- lapply(glm_poi_pvals, function(x) if (is.numeric(x)) signif(x, digits = 2) else x) 

```

## Line Graph with Notation
```{r, eval=FALSE}
perc_occluded_isa_line_plot <- ggplot(data=sum_perc_occluded_isa, aes(x=ISA,y=perc_occluded,
                                              group=dfe, color=dfe)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("black","red")) +
  geom_line(aes(linetype=dfe), size = .75) +
  geom_point(aes(shape=dfe), size = 2.5) + 
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=perc_occluded - se, 
                    ymax=perc_occluded + se), width=2) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1,30)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,100)) +
  theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12), 
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12)) +
  theme(axis.line.x = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
        axis.line.y = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.5)) +
  annotate("text", -Inf, Inf, parse = TRUE, 
           label = as.character(expression("Distance:"~"p = x")), 
           vjust=1.3, hjust=-.14, size=4) +
  annotate("text", -Inf, Inf, parse = TRUE, 
           label = as.character(expression("Site:"~"p = x")), 
           vjust=2.4, hjust=-.175, size=4) +
  annotate("text", -Inf, Inf, parse = TRUE, 
           label = as.character(expression("Site"~X~"Distance: p < x")), 
           vjust=3.5, hjust=-.105, size=4) +
  labs(x="%ISA (Impervious Surface Area)", y= expression("% Stomata Occluded")) +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14))
```

## Dynamic placement of legend
```{r}

# this finds the least dense part of the plot and places the legend there  
  
dens <- with(UNE_ABG_leafSEM_unt, kde2d(UNE_ABG_leafSEM_unt[["biomass_C_kg_m2"]], UNE_ABG_leafSEM_unt[[leaf_var_mean]], n = 50))

# Find the location of lowest density
  pos <- which(dens$z == min(dens$z), arr.ind = TRUE)
  x_pos <- dens$x[pos[1]]
  y_pos <- dens$y[pos[2]]

  legend_x <- (x_pos - min(UNE_ABG_leafSEM_unt[["biomass_C_kg_m2"]])) / diff(range(UNE_ABG_leafSEM_unt[["biomass_C_kg_m2"]]))
  legend_y <- (y_pos - min(UNE_ABG_leafSEM_unt[[leaf_var_mean]])) / diff(range(UNE_ABG_leafSEM_unt[[leaf_var_mean]]))

# add this to the ggplot
+
    theme(
      legend.position = c(legend_x, legend_y),
      legend.justification = c("right", "bottom"),
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = alpha("white", 0.7), color = NA)
    )

```
## Regression Figure with Notation
```{r, eval=FALSE}
sum_perc_occluded <- data_summary(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites,
                             varname = "perc_occluded",
                            groupnames = c("ISA", "dfe"))


perc_occluded_isa_lm_fig <- ggplot(data=sum_perc_occluded,mapping=aes(x=ISA,y=perc_occluded,col=dfe)) +
  geom_smooth(method=lm,se=FALSE)+
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3), size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=perc_occluded - se, 
                    ymax=perc_occluded + se), width=1) +
  theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12), 
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12)) +
  theme(axis.line.x = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
        axis.line.y = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.5)) +
  ylim(0,100) +
  annotate(geom = "text", x=0, y=10, label="Edge: p=0.68, R^2=0.047, m=0.0.02\nInterior: p=0.81, R^2=0.0039, m=0.012\nDistance From Edge X %ISA: p= 0.91", hjust=0) +
  xlab("% ISA (Impervious Surface Area)") +
  ylab("% Stomata Occluded") +
  guides(color = guide_legend(title = "Distance\nFrom Edge (m)")) +
  theme(axis.title=element_text(size=15))+
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#999999", "#060000"))

# dynamic label

    if(all(is.na(UNE_ABG_leafSEM_norm[[leaf_var_norm]]))=="FALSE") {
      fig <- fig +
    annotate(geom = "text", size = 4, x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.2, 
                     label=paste(
              (paste0("Edge: ",ifelse(sw_edge$p.value<=0.05, "Shapiro-Wilks < 0.05",
                      ifelse(UNE_ABG_leaf_var_edge_lm_sum$coefficients["biomass_C_kg_m2","Pr(>|t|)"]>0.001,
                               paste("p =",signif(UNE_ABG_leaf_var_edge_lm_sum$coefficients["biomass_C_kg_m2","Pr(>|t|)"],digits=3))," < 0.001")), 
                      paste0(expression(" R"^2*"= ")), signif(UNE_ABG_leaf_var_edge_lm_sum$adj.r.squared, digits =3)
                      )),
              (paste0("\nInterior: ",ifelse(sw_interior$p.value<=0.05, "Shapiro-Wilks < 0.05",
                      ifelse(UNE_ABG_leaf_var_interior_lm_sum$coefficients["biomass_C_kg_m2","Pr(>|t|)"]>0.001,
                               paste("p =",signif(UNE_ABG_leaf_var_interior_lm_sum$coefficients["biomass_C_kg_m2","Pr(>|t|)"],digits=3))," < 0.001")))), 
              (paste0("\nBoth: ",ifelse(sw$p.value<=0.05, "Shapiro-Wilks < 0.05",
                      ifelse(UNE_ABG_leaf_var_lm_sum$coefficients["biomass_C_kg_m2","Pr(>|t|)"]>0.001,
                                 paste("p =",signif(UNE_ABG_leaf_var_lm_sum$coefficients["biomass_C_kg_m2","Pr(>|t|)"],digits=3))," < 0.001")))),
              (paste0("\nAboveground Biomass x DfE: ",ifelse(UNE_ABG_leaf_var_lm_sum$coefficients["biomass_C_kg_m2:dfe90","Pr(>|t|)"]>0.001,
                    paste("p =",signif(UNE_ABG_leaf_var_lm_sum$coefficients["biomass_C_kg_m2:dfe90","Pr(>|t|)"],digits=3))," < 0.001"))))) }
```


#### Statistics
```{r}
# Testing for Normality:

  ## On perc_occluded as a whole

  qqnorm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded)
  qqline(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded)

  shapiro.test(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded) # p = 0.031, so it is NOT normal
  
  ## On the ANOVA models
  perc_occluded_dfe_aov <- aov(perc_occluded~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  shapiro.test(perc_occluded_dfe_aov$residuals)
  leveneTest(perc_occluded_dfe_aov)
  plot(perc_occluded_dfe_aov)
  
  perc_occluded_dist_aov <- aov(perc_occluded~urb_cat_dist*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  shapiro.test(perc_occluded_dist_aov$residuals)
  leveneTest(perc_occluded_dist_aov)
  plot(perc_occluded_dist_aov)
  
  perc_occluded_isa_aov <- aov(perc_occluded~urb_cat_isa*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  shapiro.test(perc_occluded_isa_aov$residuals)

# Transforming the Data

  ## original distribution
  hist(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded)
  perc_occluded_transform <-LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded # creates a new copy of the vector so we can get it ready to transform

  ## log transform

  # because we can't take the log of 0, we need to add 1 to all zero values of this data (log(1) is zero)
   perc_occluded_log_transform <- ifelse(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded == 0, 1, LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded) # replaces all 0s with 1s 
   log_perc_occluded <- log10(perc_occluded_log_transform) # log transforms the data
   hist(log_perc_occluded)
   shapiro.test(log_perc_occluded) # p = 0.093

  ## square root transform
  sqrt_perc_occluded <- sqrt(perc_occluded_transform)
  hist(sqrt_perc_occluded)
  shapiro.test(sqrt_perc_occluded) # p = 0.074

  ## inverse transform
  perc_occluded_inv_transform <- LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded + 0.00001
  inv_perc_occluded <- 1/(perc_occluded_inv_transform)
  hist(inv_perc_occluded)
  shapiro.test(inv_perc_occluded) # p = 0.0089

    ### of these transformations, LOG TRANSFORMATION produces the highest p-value

# Analysis of Variance (Parametric)

  LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$log_perc_occluded <- log10(perc_occluded_transform) # to run an anova on the transformed variable, we must add it into the larger data frame

  ## perc_occluded, transformed: Edge vs Interior
  log_perc_occluded_dfe_aov <- aov(log_perc_occluded~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(log_perc_occluded_dfe_aov)

   # p = 0.67

  ## perc_occluded, transformed: Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by dist to Boston)

  log_perc_occluded_dist_aov <- aov(log_perc_occluded~urb_cat_dist*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(log_perc_occluded_dist_aov)

    # Urban vs. Rural (dist): p = 0.93
    # Interaction (dist): p = 0.26

  ## perc_occluded, transformed: Edge vs Interior & Urban vs. Rural (by %ISA)

  log_perc_occluded_isa_aov <- aov(log_perc_occluded~urb_cat_isa*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(log_perc_occluded_isa_aov)

    # Urban vs. Rural (ISA): p = 0.82
    # Interaction (ISA): p = 0.67
  
 TukeyHSD(log_perc_occluded_isa_aov)

```

#### Statistics 

```{r}
# Testing for Normality

qqnorm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_PM)
qqline(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_PM)

shapiro.test(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_PM) # p = 0.1411

  # the data is normal enough that we can perform statistics on the untransformed data - yay

# Analysis of Variance (Parametric)

  ## Edge vs Interior
  perc_occluded_PM_dfe_aov <- aov(perc_occluded_PM~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(perc_occluded_PM_dfe_aov)

  # p = 0.62

  ## Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by dist to Boston)

  perc_occluded_PM_dist_aov <- aov(perc_occluded_PM~urb_cat_dist*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(perc_occluded_PM_dist_aov)

  # Edge vs. Interior: p = 0.62
  # Urban vs. Rural (dist): p = 0.99
  # Interaction (dist): p = 0.35

  ## Edge vs Interior & Urban vs. Rural (by %ISA)

  perc_occluded_PM_isa_aov <- aov(perc_occluded_PM~urb_cat_isa*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(perc_occluded_PM_isa_aov)

  # Edge vs. Interior: p = 0.62
  # Urban vs. Rural (ISA): p = 0.41
  # Interaction (ISA): p = 0.81
  
TukeyHSD(perc_occluded_PM_isa_aov)

```

#### Statistics
```{r}

# Testing for Normality

qqnorm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_hyphae)
qqline(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_hyphae)

shapiro.test(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_hyphae) # p = 0.0030

# Transforming the Data

  ## original distribution:
  hist(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_hyphae)
  perc_occluded_hyphae_transform <-LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_hyphae # creates a new copy of the vector so we can get it ready to transform

  ## log transform
  # because we can't take the log of 0, we need to add 1 to all zero values of this data (log(1) is zero)
  perc_occluded_hyphae_log_transform <- ifelse(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_hyphae == 0, 1, LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_hyphae) # replaces all 0s with 1s
  log_perc_occluded_hyphae <- log10(perc_occluded_hyphae_log_transform) # log transforms the data
  hist(log_perc_occluded_hyphae)
  shapiro.test(log_perc_occluded_hyphae) # p = 0.0005574

    ### Q-Q Plot for log-transformed data
    qqnorm(log_perc_occluded_hyphae)
    qqline(log_perc_occluded_hyphae) # doesn't look like it's a single point driving non-normality

  ## square root transform
  sqrt_perc_occluded_hyphae <- sqrt(perc_occluded_hyphae_transform)
  hist(sqrt_perc_occluded_hyphae)
  shapiro.test(sqrt_perc_occluded_hyphae) # p = 0.07024

  ## inverse transform
  perc_occluded_hyphae_inv_transform <- LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_hyphae + 0.00001
  inv_perc_occluded_hyphae <- 1/(perc_occluded_hyphae_inv_transform)
  hist(inv_perc_occluded_hyphae)
  shapiro.test(inv_perc_occluded_hyphae) # p = 5.803e-10

    ### of these transformations, SQUARE ROOT TRANSFORMATION produces the highest p-value

# Analysis of Variance (parametric)

LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$sqrt_perc_occluded_hyphae <- sqrt(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_hyphae) # to run an anova on the transformed variable, we must add it into the larger data frame

  ## Edge vs Interior
  sqrt_perc_occluded_hyphae_dfe_aov <- aov(sqrt_perc_occluded_hyphae~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(sqrt_perc_occluded_hyphae_dfe_aov)

  # p = 0.31

  ## Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by dist to Boston)

  sqrt_perc_occluded_hyphae_dist_aov <- aov(sqrt_perc_occluded_hyphae~urb_cat_dist*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(sqrt_perc_occluded_hyphae_dist_aov)

  # Edge vs. Interior: p = 0.2996 
  # Urban vs. Rural (dist): p = 0.5200
  # Interaction (dist): p = 0.0916

  ## Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by %ISA)

sqrt_perc_occluded_hyphae_isa_aov <- aov(sqrt_perc_occluded_hyphae~urb_cat_isa*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
summary(sqrt_perc_occluded_hyphae_isa_aov)

TukeyHSD(sqrt_perc_occluded_hyphae_isa_aov,conf.level = 0.95)

# Edge vs. Interior: p = 0.2939
# Urban vs. Rural (ISA): p = 0.0699
# Interaction (ISA): p = 0.4129
```

```{r}
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
        aspect.ratio = 1,
        axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black"))+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("grey","black"))

# Urban vs Rural (by %ISA), Edge vs Interior 
sum_perc_occluded_single_cell_urb_cat_isa <- data_summary(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites,
                             varname = "perc_occluded_single_cell",
                            groupnames = c("urb_cat_isa", "dfe"))

perc_occluded_single_cell_urb_cat_isa <-
ggplot(sum_perc_occluded_single_cell_urb_cat_isa,mapping=aes(x=urb_cat_isa,y=perc_occluded_single_cell,col=dfe)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3), size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = perc_occluded_single_cell - se, ymax = perc_occluded_single_cell + se), 
                width = 0.2, 
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  labs(title = "Single-Cell Microbes",
       x = "Site Type by %ISA", 
       y = "% Stomata Occluded") +
  ylim(0,30)+
  theme_minimal() +   
  guides(color = guide_legend(title = "Distance\nFrom Edge (m)")) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
        aspect.ratio = 1,
        axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black"))+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("grey","black"))

# putting both plots together
perc_occluded_single_cell_cat_figs <- plot_grid(perc_occluded_single_cell_urb_cat_dist, perc_occluded_single_cell_urb_cat_isa,labels=NULL,ncol=2,rel_widths=c(1,1.45),label_size = 12)

```

#### Statistics

```{r}
# Testing for Normality

qqnorm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_single_cell)
qqline(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_single_cell)

shapiro.test(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_single_cell) # p = 1.069e-09

# Transforming the Data

  ## original distribution:
  hist(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_single_cell)
  perc_occluded_single_cell_transform <-LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_single_cell # creates a new copy of the vector so we can get it ready to transform

  ## log transform
  # because we can't take the log of 0, we need to add 1 to all zero values of this data (log(1) is zero)
  perc_occluded_single_cell_log_transform <- ifelse(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_single_cell == 0, 1, LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_single_cell) # replaces all 0s with 1s 
  log_perc_occluded_single_cell <- log10(perc_occluded_single_cell_log_transform) # log transforms the data
  hist(log_perc_occluded_single_cell)
  shapiro.test(log_perc_occluded_single_cell) # p = 6.757e-06

  ## square root transform
  sqrt_perc_occluded_single_cell <- sqrt(perc_occluded_single_cell_transform)
  hist(sqrt_perc_occluded_single_cell)
  shapiro.test(sqrt_perc_occluded_single_cell) # p = 2.78e-06

  ## inverse transform
  inv_perc_occluded_single_cell <- LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_single_cell + 0.00001
  hist(inv_perc_occluded_single_cell)
  shapiro.test(inv_perc_occluded_single_cell) # p = 1.069e-09

  ## natural log transform
  ln_perc_occluded_single_cell <- log(perc_occluded_single_cell_transform)
  hist(ln_perc_occluded_single_cell)
  shapiro.test(ln_perc_occluded_single_cell) # p = 6.757e-06

# trying to remove a couple outliers - incomplete, but can try later
# log_perc_occluded_single_cell_31 <- log_perc_occluded_single_cell[X:32] # this removes rows, if they look outliery
# shapiro.test(log_perc_occluded_PM_31) # p = 0.2097
```

#### Statistics

```{r}
# Testing for Normality

qqnorm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_other)
qqline(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_other)

shapiro.test(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_other) # p = 1.213e-08

# Transforming the Data

  ## original distribution:
  hist(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_other)
  perc_occluded_other_transform <-LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_other # creates a new copy of the vector so we can get it ready to log-transform

  ## log transform
  # because we can't take the log of 0, we need to add 1 to all zero values of this data (log(1) is zero)
  perc_occluded_other_log_transform <- ifelse(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_other == 0, 1, LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_other) # replaces all 0s with 1s  
  log_perc_occluded_other <- log10(perc_occluded_other_log_transform) # log transforms the data
  hist(log_perc_occluded_other)
  shapiro.test(log_perc_occluded_other) # p = 0.001914

  ## square root transform
  sqrt_perc_occluded_other <- sqrt(perc_occluded_other_transform)
  hist(sqrt_perc_occluded_other)
  shapiro.test(sqrt_perc_occluded_other) # p = 0.001672

  ## inverse transform
  perc_occluded_other_inv_transform <- LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$perc_occluded_other + 0.00001
  inv_perc_occluded_other <- 1/(perc_occluded_other_inv_transform)
  hist(inv_perc_occluded_other)
  shapiro.test(inv_perc_occluded_other) # p = 7.222e-09

```

#### Statistics
```{r}
# Testing for Normality

hist(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$stomatal_density)

qqnorm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$stomatal_density)
qqline(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$stomatal_density)

shapiro.test(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$stomatal_density) # p = 0.43

# data are normal, so no transformations needed!

# Analyses of Variance

  ## Edge vs Interior
  stomatal_density_dfe_aov <- aov(stomatal_density~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(stomatal_density_dfe_aov)

  # Edge vs. Interior: p = 0.80

  ## Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by dist to Boston)

  stomatal_density_dist_aov <- aov(stomatal_density~urb_cat_dist*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(stomatal_density_dist_aov)

  # Urban vs. Rural (dist): p = 0.59
  # Interaction (dist): p = 0.28

  ## Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by %ISA)

  stomatal_density_isa_aov <- aov(stomatal_density~urb_cat_isa*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(stomatal_density_isa_aov)

  # Urban vs. Rural (ISA): p = 0.76
  # Interaction (ISA): p = 0.35
  
```


#### Statistics
```{r}
# Testing for Normality

qqnorm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$hyphal_conc)
qqline(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$hyphal_conc)

shapiro.test(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$hyphal_conc) # p = 0.00068

  ## Parametric ANOVA Assumptions Checking
    
    ### Hyphal Conc: Edge vs Interior
    hyphal_conc_dfe_aov <- aov(hyphal_conc~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
    shapiro.test(hyphal_conc_dfe_aov$residuals)
    leveneTest(hyphal_conc_dfe_aov)
    plot(hyphal_conc_dfe_aov)
    
    ### Hyphal Conc: Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by dist to Boston)
    
    hyphal_conc_dist_aov <- aov(hyphal_conc~urb_cat_dist*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
    shapiro.test(hyphal_conc_dist_aov$residuals)
    leveneTest(hyphal_conc_dist_aov)

    ### Hyphal Conc: Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by %ISA)
    
    hyphal_conc_isa_aov <- aov(hyphal_conc~urb_cat_isa*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
    shapiro.test(hyphal_conc_isa_aov$residuals)
    leveneTest(hyphal_conc_isa_aov)

## Transforming the Data

    ### original distribution:
    hist(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$hyphal_conc)
    hyphal_conc_transform <-LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$hyphal_conc # creates a new copy of the vector so we can get it ready to transform
    
    ### log transform
      #### because we can't take the log of 0, we will replace all zeroes with ones (log(1) is zero)
    
    hyphal_conc_log_transform <- ifelse(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$hyphal_conc == 0, 1, LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$hyphal_conc) # replaces all 0s with 1s
    log_hyphal_conc <- log10(hyphal_conc_log_transform) # log transforms the data
    hist(log_hyphal_conc)
    shapiro.test(log_hyphal_conc) # p = 0.008201 ****
    
      #### Q-Q plots for log-transformed data
      qqnorm(log_hyphal_conc)
      qqline(log_hyphal_conc)
    
    ### square root transform
    sqrt_hyphal_conc <- sqrt(hyphal_conc_transform)
    hist(sqrt_hyphal_conc)
    shapiro.test(sqrt_hyphal_conc) # p = 0.1587
    
    ### inverse transform
    hyphal_conc_inv_transform <- LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$hyphal_conc + 0.00001
    inv_hyphal_conc <- 1/(hyphal_conc_inv_transform)
    hist(inv_hyphal_conc)
    shapiro.test(inv_hyphal_conc) # p = 2.014e-10
    
    #### of these transformations, SQUARE ROOT TRANSFORMATION produces the highest p-value

## Analyses of Variance (Parametric) on Sqrt-transformed Data

    LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$sqrt_hyphal_conc <- sqrt(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$hyphal_conc) # to run an anova on the transformed variable, we must add it into the larger data frame
    
    ### Hyphal Conc: Edge vs Interior
    sqrt_hyphal_conc_dfe_aov <- aov(sqrt_hyphal_conc~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
    summary(sqrt_hyphal_conc_dfe_aov)
    
      # Edge vs. Interior: p = 0.18
    
    ### Hyphal Conc, transformed: Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by dist to Boston)
    
    sqrt_hyphal_conc_dist_aov <- aov(sqrt_hyphal_conc~urb_cat_dist*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
    summary(sqrt_hyphal_conc_dist_aov)
    
      # Urban vs. Rural (dist): p = 0.5482
      # Interaction (dist): p = 0.0794
    
    ### Hyphal Conc, transformed: Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by %ISA)
    
    sqrt_hyphal_conc_isa_aov <- aov(sqrt_hyphal_conc~urb_cat_isa*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
    summary(sqrt_hyphal_conc_isa_aov)
    
      # Urban vs. Rural (ISA): p = 0.120
      # Interaction (ISA): p = 0.324
    
    #### because these differences don't seem significant, I will do a "gut check" on the most significant-looking comparison: edge vs interior in the urban sites (defined by %ISA).
    
    ### ANOVA on Urban Sites Only
    
    ### Hyphal Conc: Edge vs Interior (Urban sites by isa only, transformed w sqrt)
    
    LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_urban_isa <- subset(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites, urb_cat_isa=="Urban")
    LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_urban_isa$dfe<-as.factor(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_urban_isa$dfe)
    LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_urban_isa$urb_cat_isa<-as.factor(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_urban_isa$urb_cat_isa)
    
    sqrt_hyphal_conc_dfe_aov_urban <- aov(sqrt_hyphal_conc~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_urban_isa)
    summary(sqrt_hyphal_conc_dfe_aov_urban) # p = 0.14
    
    
    ### Hyphal Conc: Edge vs Interior (Urban sites by dist only, transformed w sqrt)
    
    LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_urban_dist <- subset(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites, urb_cat_dist=="Urban")
    LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_urban_dist$dfe<-as.factor(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_urban_dist$dfe)
    LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_urban_dist$urb_cat_dist<-as.factor(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_urban_dist$urb_cat_dist)
    
    ### Graph of Transformed Variable (urban by ISA only)
    
    sum_hyphal_conc_urb_only <- sum_hyphal_conc_urb_cat_isa <- data_summary(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_urban_isa,
                                 varname = "sqrt_hyphal_conc",
                                groupnames = c("urb_cat_isa", "dfe"))
    
    hyphal_conc_urb_only <-
      ggplot(sum_hyphal_conc_urb_only,mapping=aes(x=urb_cat_isa,y=sqrt_hyphal_conc,col=dfe)) +
      geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3), size = 3) +
      geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = sqrt_hyphal_conc - se, ymax = sqrt_hyphal_conc + se), 
                    width = 0.2, 
                    position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
      labs(title = "",
           x = "Site Type by %ISA", 
           y = expression("sqrt(hyphae (nm/"*mu*"m"^-2* " leaf))")) +
      theme_minimal() +   
      theme(legend.position="none") +
      ylim(0,7) +
      # guides(color = guide_legend(title = "Distance\nFrom Edge (m)")) +
      theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
            panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
            axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
            aspect.ratio = 1,
            axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black"),
            axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black"))+
      scale_color_manual(values = c("grey","black"))

## ANOVA on Untransformed Data  **not for use**

    ### Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (%ISA)
    
    hyphal_conc_untr_dist_aov_urban_isa <- aov(hyphal_conc~urb_cat_isa*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
    summary(hyphal_conc_untr_dist_aov_urban_isa)
    
    
    ### Non-parametric ANOVA on Urban Sites Only

# By %ISA

  ### Hyphal Conc: Edge vs Interior 
  
  hyphal_conc_dfe_aovART_urban_dist <- art(hyphal_conc~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_urban_dist)
  anova(hyphal_conc_dfe_aovART_urban_dist)

# By %ISA

    ### Hyphal Conc: Edge vs Interior 
    
    hyphal_conc_dfe_aovART_urban_isa <- art(hyphal_conc~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_urban_isa)
    anova(hyphal_conc_dfe_aovART_urban_isa)

# printing both plots
plot_grid(hyphal_conc_urb_cat_dist, hyphal_conc_urb_cat_isa,labels=NULL,ncol=2)

# printing just urban (with the variable transformed)
plot_grid(hyphal_conc_urb_only)

# ART ANOVA on all sites

      ### Edge vs Interior
      hyphal_conc_dfe_aovART <- art(hyphal_conc~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
      anova(hyphal_conc_dfe_aovART)
      
      # p = 0.21

      anova(hyphal_conc_dist_aovART)
```

### Single-Cell Density
#### Figures
```{r}
# Urban vs Rural (by dist from Boston), Edge vs Interior 
sum_single_cell_density_urb_cat_dist <- data_summary(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites,
                             varname = "single_cell_density",
                            groupnames = c("urb_cat_dist", "dfe"))

single_cell_density_urb_cat_dist <-
  ggplot(data=sum_single_cell_density_urb_cat_dist,mapping=aes(x=urb_cat_dist,y=single_cell_density,col=dfe)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("grey","black")) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3), size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = single_cell_density - se, ymax = single_cell_density + se), 
                width = 0.2, 
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  labs(title = "",
       x = "Site Type by Distance from Boston", 
       y = expression("single-cell microbes (mm"^-2*" leaf)")) +
  theme_minimal() +   
  theme(legend.position="none") +
  ylim(0,1800) +
  # guides(color = guide_legend(title = "Distance\nFrom Edge (m)")) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
        aspect.ratio = 1,
        axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black"))


# Urban vs Rural (by %ISA), Edge vs Interior

                            groupnames = c("urb_cat_isa", "dfe"))

single_cell_density_urb_cat_isa <-
  ggplot(sum_single_cell_density_urb_cat_isa,mapping=aes(x=urb_cat_isa,y=single_cell_density,col=dfe)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("grey","black")) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3), size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = single_cell_density - se, ymax = single_cell_density + se), 
                width = 0.2, 
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  labs(title = "",
       x = "Site Type by %ISA", 
       y = expression("single-cell microbes (mm"^-2*" leaf)")) +
  theme_minimal() +

  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),

```

#### Statistics
```{r}
# Testing for Normality

  # paste both these lines into console to visualize
  qqnorm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$single_cell_density)
  qqline(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$single_cell_density)
  
  shapiro.test(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$single_cell_density) # p = 8.524e-07

# Transforming the Data

  ## original distribution:
  hist(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$single_cell_density)
  single_cell_density_transform <-LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$single_cell_density # creates a new copy of the vector so we can get it ready to transform

  ## log transform
  # because we can't take the log of 0, we need to add 1 to all zero values of this data (log(1) is zero)
  single_cell_density_log_transform <- ifelse(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$single_cell_density == 0, 1, LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$single_cell_density) # replaces all 0s with 1s  
  log_single_cell_density <- log10(single_cell_density_log_transform) # log transforms the data
  hist(log_single_cell_density)
  shapiro.test(log_single_cell_density) # p = 0.011

    # Q-Q plots for log-transformed data
    qqnorm(log_single_cell_density)
    qqline(log_single_cell_density)

  ## square root transform
  sqrt_single_cell_density <- sqrt(single_cell_density_transform)
  hist(sqrt_single_cell_density)
  shapiro.test(sqrt_single_cell_density) # p = 1.611e-06

  ## inverse transform
  single_cell_density_inv_transform <- LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$single_cell_density + 0.00001
  inv_single_cell_density <- 1/single_cell_density_inv_transform
  hist(inv_single_cell_density)
  shapiro.test(inv_single_cell_density) # p = 2.014e-10

  ## natural log transform
  ln_single_cell_density <- log(single_cell_density_log_transform)
  hist(ln_single_cell_density)
  shapiro.test(ln_single_cell_density) # p = 0.01131

# Experimenting with outlier removal  
  LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_sc_outliers_removed <- LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites[c(1,3:9,11:32),]
  
  # Testing for Normality

  # paste both these lines into console to visualize
  qqnorm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_sc_outliers_removed$single_cell_density)
  qqline(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites_sc_outliers_removed$single_cell_density)

```

#### Statistics

```{r}

# Testing for Normality

qqnorm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$wax)
qqline(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$wax)

shapiro.test(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$wax) # p = 0.0004921

# Transforming the Data

  ## original distribution:
  hist(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$wax)

  wax_transform <-LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$wax 
  # creates a new copy of the vector so we can get it ready to transform

  ## log transform
  # because we can't take the log of 0, we need to add 1 to all zero values of this data (log(1) is zero)
  wax_log_transform <- ifelse(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$wax == 0, 1, LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$wax) # replaces all 0s with 1s  
  log_wax <- log10(wax_log_transform) # log transforms the data
  hist(log_wax)
  shapiro.test(log_wax) # p = 1.707e-05

    # Q-Q plots for log-transformed data
    qqnorm(log_wax)
    qqline(log_wax)

  ## square root transform
  sqrt_wax <- sqrt(wax_transform)
  hist(sqrt_wax)
  shapiro.test(sqrt_wax) # p = 0.0001339

  ## inverse transform
  wax_inv_transform <- LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$wax + 0.00001
  inv_wax <- 1/(wax_inv_transform)
  hist(inv_wax)
  shapiro.test(inv_wax) # p = 3.917e-08

```

```{r}
foliar_PM2.5_urb_cat_isa <-

  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3), size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = foliar_PM2.5 - se, ymax = foliar_PM2.5 + se),

```

#### Statistics
```{r}
# Testing for Normality

  ## on untransformed variable
  qqnorm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$foliar_PM2.5)
  qqline(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$foliar_PM2.5)
  shapiro.test(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$foliar_PM2.5) # p = 8.1 e-10
  
# Transforming the Data

  ## original distribution:
  hist(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$foliar_PM2.5)
  foliar_PM2.5_transform <- LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$foliar_PM2.5 # creates a new copy of the vector so we can get it ready to transform

  ## log transform
  # because we can't take the log of 0, we need to add 1 to all zero values of this data (log(1) is zero)
  foliar_PM2.5_log_transform <- (foliar_PM2.5_transform)*10000 # since these numbers are all less than 1, their logs will be negative, so I am multiplying them by 10000 to make sure they are all above 0
  foliar_PM2.5_log_transform <- ifelse(foliar_PM2.5_log_transform  == 0, 1, foliar_PM2.5_log_transform) # replaces all 0s with 1s  
  log_foliar_PM2.5 <- log10(foliar_PM2.5_log_transform) # log transforms the data
  hist(log_foliar_PM2.5)
  shapiro.test(log_foliar_PM2.5) # p = 0.2531

  ## square root transform
  sqrt_foliar_PM2.5 <- sqrt(foliar_PM2.5_transform)
  hist(sqrt_foliar_PM2.5)
  shapiro.test(sqrt_foliar_PM2.5) # p = 1.647e-06

  ## inverse transform
  foliar_PM2.5_inv_transform <- LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$foliar_PM2.5 + 0.00001
  inv_foliar_PM2.5 <- 1/(foliar_PM2.5_inv_transform)
  hist(inv_foliar_PM2.5)
  shapiro.test(inv_foliar_PM2.5) # p = 1.544e-05

# Analysis of Variance (parametric)

LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$log_foliar_PM2.5 <- log_foliar_PM2.5 # to run an anova on the transformed variable, we must add it into the larger data frame

  ## Edge vs Interior
  log_foliar_PM2.5_dfe_aov <- aov(log_foliar_PM2.5~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(log_foliar_PM2.5_dfe_aov)
  
    # Edge vs Interior: p = 0.89

  ## Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by dist to Boston)

    log_foliar_PM2.5_dist_aov <- aov(log_foliar_PM2.5~urb_cat_dist*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
    summary(log_foliar_PM2.5_dist_aov)

    # Urban vs. Rural (dist): p = 1.0
    # Interaction (dist): p = 0.98

  ## Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by %ISA)

    log_foliar_PM2.5_isa_aov <- aov(log_foliar_PM2.5~urb_cat_isa*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
    summary(log_foliar_PM2.5_isa_aov)

    # Urban vs. Rural (ISA): p = 0.019
    # Interaction (ISA): p = 0.62

```

#### Statistics

```{r}
# Testing for Normality

 ## on untransformed variable
  qqnorm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$foliar_PM10)
  qqline(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$foliar_PM10)

shapiro.test(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$foliar_PM10) # p = 9.3e-09

# Transforming the Data

  ## original distribution:
  hist(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$foliar_PM10)

  foliar_PM10_transform <-LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$foliar_PM10 # creates a new copy of the vector so we can get it ready to transform

  ## log transform
  foliar_PM10_log_transform <- (foliar_PM10_transform)*10000 # since some of these numbers are less than 1, their logs will be negative, so I am multiplying them by 10000 to make sure they are all above 0
  foliar_PM10_log_transform <- ifelse(foliar_PM10_log_transform  == 0, 1, foliar_PM10_log_transform) # replaces all 0s with 1s  
  log_foliar_PM10 <- log10(foliar_PM10_log_transform) # log transforms the data
  hist(log_foliar_PM10)
  shapiro.test(log_foliar_PM10) # p = 0.75

  qqnorm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$foliar_PM10)
  qqline(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$foliar_PM10)

  ## square root transform
  sqrt_foliar_PM10 <- sqrt(foliar_PM10_transform)
  hist(sqrt_foliar_PM10)
  shapiro.test(sqrt_foliar_PM10) # p = 2.3 e-05

  ## inverse transform
  foliar_PM10_inv_transform <- LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$foliar_PM10 + 0.00001
  inv_foliar_PM10 <- 1/foliar_PM10_inv_transform
  hist(inv_foliar_PM10)
  shapiro.test(inv_foliar_PM10) # p = 2.9 e-07

# Analysis of Variance (parametric) 

LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$log_foliar_PM10 <- log_foliar_PM10 # to run an anova on the transformed variable, we must add it into the larger data frame

  ## Edge vs Interior
  log_foliar_PM10_dfe_aov <- aov(log_foliar_PM10~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(log_foliar_PM10_dfe_aov)

  # Edge vs. Interior: p = 0.80

  ## Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by dist to Boston)

  log_foliar_PM10_dist_aov <- aov(log_foliar_PM10~urb_cat_dist*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(log_foliar_PM10_dist_aov)

  # Urban vs. Rural (dist): p = 0.36
  # Interaction (dist): p = 0.90

  ## Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by %ISA)

  log_foliar_PM10_isa_aov <- aov(log_foliar_PM10~urb_cat_isa*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(log_foliar_PM10_isa_aov)

  # Urban vs. Rural (ISA): p = 0.11
  # Interaction (ISA): p = 0.64
```

```{r}
# Testing for Normality

qqnorm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM2.5)
qqline(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM2.5)

shapiro.test(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM2.5) # p = 5.1e-05

# Transforming the Data

  ## original distribution:
  hist(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM2.5)

  ambient_PM2.5_transform <-LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM2.5 # creates a new copy of the vector so we can get it ready to transform

  ## log transform
  # because we can't take the log of 0, we need to add 1 to all zero values of this data (log(1) is zero)
  ambient_PM2.5_log_transform <- ifelse(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM2.5  == 0, 1, LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM2.5) # replaces all 0s with 1s  
  log_ambient_PM2.5 <- log10(ambient_PM2.5_log_transform) # log transforms the data
  hist(log_ambient_PM2.5)
  shapiro.test(log_ambient_PM2.5) # p = 0.59

  ## square root transform
  sqrt_ambient_PM2.5 <- sqrt(ambient_PM2.5_transform)
  hist(sqrt_ambient_PM2.5)
  shapiro.test(sqrt_ambient_PM2.5) # p = 0.012

  ## inverse transform
  ambient_PM2.5_inv_transform <- LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM2.5 + 0.00001
  inv_ambient_PM2.5 <- 1/ambient_PM2.5_inv_transform
  hist(inv_ambient_PM2.5)
  shapiro.test(inv_ambient_PM2.5) # p = 0.14

# Analyses of Variance 

LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$log_ambient_PM2.5 <- log_ambient_PM2.5 # to run an anova on the transformed variable, we must add it into the larger data frame

  ## Edge vs Interior
  log_ambient_PM2.5_dfe_aov <- aov(log_ambient_PM2.5~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(log_ambient_PM2.5_dfe_aov)

  # Edge vs. Interior: p = 0.49

  ## Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by dist to Boston)

  log_ambient_PM2.5_dist_aov <- aov(log_ambient_PM2.5~urb_cat_dist*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(log_ambient_PM2.5_dist_aov)

  # Urban vs. Rural (dist): p = 0.10
  # Interaction (dist): p = 0.47

  ## Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by %ISA)

  log_ambient_PM2.5_isa_aov <- aov(log_ambient_PM2.5~urb_cat_isa*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  summary(log_ambient_PM2.5_isa_aov)

  # Urban vs. Rural (ISA): p = 0.091
  # Interaction (ISA): p = 0.85
```

### Ambient PM10
#### Figures
```{r}

# Urban vs Rural (by Distance to Boston), Edge vs Interior 

sum_ambient_PM10_urb_cat_dist <- data_summary(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites,
                             varname = "ambient_PM10",
                            groupnames = c("dfe", "urb_cat_dist"))
ambient_PM10_urb_cat_dist <-
  ggplot(sum_ambient_PM10_urb_cat_dist,mapping=aes(x=urb_cat_dist,y=ambient_PM10,col=dfe)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3), size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ambient_PM10 - se, ymax = ambient_PM10 + se), 
                width = 0.2, 
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  labs(title = expression("PM"[10]),
       x = "Site Type by Distance from Boston", 
       y = expression("PM"[10]*" Concentration ("*mu*g/m^3*")"))+
  theme_minimal() +   
  ylim(0,40)+
  theme(legend.position="none") +
  # guides(color = guide_legend(title = "Distance\nFrom Edge (m)")) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
        aspect.ratio = 1,
        axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black"))+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("grey","black"))

# Urban vs Rural (by %ISA), Edge vs Interior 

sum_ambient_PM10_urb_cat_isa <- data_summary(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites,
                             varname = "ambient_PM10",
                            groupnames = c("dfe", "urb_cat_isa"))
ambient_PM10_urb_cat_isa <-
  ggplot(sum_ambient_PM10_urb_cat_isa,mapping=aes(x=urb_cat_isa,y=ambient_PM10,col=dfe)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3), size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ambient_PM10 - se, ymax = ambient_PM10 + se), 
                width = 0.2, 
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  labs(title = expression("PM"[10]),
       x = "Site Type (%ISA)", 
       y = expression("PM"[10]*" Concentration ("*mu*g/m^3*")")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  ylim(0,40)+
  guides(color = guide_legend(title = "Distance\nFrom Edge (m)")) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
        aspect.ratio = 1,
        axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black"))+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("grey","black"))

#putting them both together

ambient_PM10_cat_figs <- plot_grid(ambient_PM10_urb_cat_dist,ambient_PM10_urb_cat_isa,labels=NULL,ncol=2,rel_widths=c(1,1.45))

```

#### Statistics 

```{r}
# Testing for Normality

qqnorm(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM10)
qqline(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM10)

shapiro.test(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM10) # p = 8.5e-07

# Transforming the Data

  ## original distribution:
  hist(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM10)

ambient_PM10_transform <- LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM10
# creates a new copy of the vector so we can get it ready to transform

  ## log transform
  # because we can't take the log of 0, we need to add 1 to all zero values of this data (log(1) is zero)
ambient_PM10_log_transform <- ifelse(LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM10  == 0, 1, LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM10) # replaces all 0s with 1s  
log_ambient_PM10 <- log10(ambient_PM10_log_transform) # log transforms the data
hist(log_ambient_PM10)
shapiro.test(log_ambient_PM10) # p = 0.00014

  ## square root transform
  sqrt_ambient_PM10 <- sqrt(ambient_PM10_transform)
  hist(sqrt_ambient_PM10)
  shapiro.test(sqrt_ambient_PM10) # p = 1.0e-05

  ## inverse transform
  ambient_PM10_inv_transform <- LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites$ambient_PM10 + 0.00001
  inv_ambient_PM10 <- 1/ambient_PM10_inv_transform
  hist(inv_ambient_PM10)
  shapiro.test(inv_ambient_PM10) # p = 0.012

# Analysis of Variance (non-parametric)

# because these data are not normal, I am using an Aligned Ranks Anova method from Steve (found in the file "Nonparametric stats_forEmma.Rmd")

library(ARTool)

  ## Edge vs Interior
  ambient_PM10_dfe_aovART <- art(ambient_PM10~dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  anova(ambient_PM10_dfe_aovART)

  # p = 0.62 

  ## Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by dist to Boston)

  ambient_PM10_dist_aovART <- art(ambient_PM10~urb_cat_dist*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  anova(ambient_PM10_dist_aovART)

  # Urban vs. Rural (dist): p = 0.44   
  # Interaction (dist): p = 0.73 

  ## Edge vs Interior; Urban vs. Rural (by %ISA)

  ambient_PM10_isa_aovART <- art(ambient_PM10~urb_cat_isa*dfe, data = LeafSEM_resp_varsxUNESites)
  anova(ambient_PM10_isa_aovART)

  # Urban vs. Rural (ISA): p = 0.42
  # Interaction (ISA): p = 0.61
  
```
